explanation: The Sigma rule specifically targets the 'http://' and 'https://' strings in the command line. An attacker can bypass this by using a UNC path to fetch the remote payload via SMB or WebDAV, which achieves the same arbitrary file download capability without triggering the HTTP-based string match.

Because the rule's selection_http condition is strictly limited to HTTP and HTTPS protocols, using the FTP protocol handler allows the Office application to download the file while evading the detection logic.

Using the file:// protocol URI scheme pointing to a remote server forces the application to resolve the remote resource (often falling back to SMB) without explicitly writing 'http://' or 'https://' in the command line.
